**AEK Handball Club** is the handball department of the major Greek multi-[sports club](/wiki/Sports_club "Sports club") [A.E.K.](/wiki/A.E.K._(sports_club) "A.E.K. (sports club)") It was established in 2005 and it is the most successful Greek club in European handball competitions, as it won the European Challenge Cup in 2021 and was also a runner-up in 2018 and 2025. The club is commonly known in European competitions as **AEK Athens HC**.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=AEK_H.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The department Handball of A.E.K. was founded by a decision of the General Assembly of A.E.K. on 12 July 2005.

On 5 August 2005, the Greek Handball Federation accepted a merger through the absorption of the association's Board of Handball GA Ilioupolis with AEK Handball Club.

During the period 2006–07, AEK Handball Club acquired the 3rd position in A1 ([Greek Men's handball championship](/wiki/Greek_Men%27s_handball_championship "Greek Men's handball championship")) and gained participation at European Cups for the first time.

During the period 2007–08, AEK participated in [EHF Cup](/wiki/EHF_Cup "EHF Cup") (European Handball Federation Cup) and was eliminated by Dinamo Baumit Bucuresti in the second round.

On 31 May 2009, with Nikos Georgantzoglou as president, AEK won the first [Greek handball cup](/wiki/Greek_men%27s_handball_cup "Greek men's handball cup") winning Filippos by a score of 33–31 in Serres.

For the period 2009–10 AEK participated at [EHF Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/EHF_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "EHF Cup Winners' Cup").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

For the third round of [EHF Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/EHF_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "EHF Cup Winners' Cup"), AEK HC played against BSB Izmir on 14 November 2009 in Athens (losing 24–29) and on 21 November 2009 in Smyrni (drawing 22–22).
During the period 2009–10, AEK HC terminated at 4th position of A1, gaining its European participation for the 2nd consecutive time and for 3rd in general.
In addition, AEK HC participated in the final four of [Greek men's handball cup](/wiki/Greek_men%27s_handball_cup "Greek men's handball cup") on 29 May 2010 in Lamia, but the semi-final game against PAOK did not take place due to fan "fights".

For the second round of the European Challenge Cup (2010–11), AEK HC won [HC Dinamo Minsk](/wiki/HC_Dinamo_Minsk_(handball) "HC Dinamo Minsk (handball)") 31–25 in Athens and lost 32–27 in Minsk and therefore AEK has been advanced to the top 16 teams.
For the third round (phase of 16), AEK defeated Sporting Lisboa at penalties. AEK lost 27–23 in Lisboa but won 27–23 in Athens and so the game went on penalty-kicks, where AEK advanced
to the top 8 with the final score of 32–27. For the Quarter-Final (phase 8),
AEK played against [Partizan](/wiki/RK_Partizan "RK Partizan") of Beograd and was disqualified.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On 7 May 2011, AEK won the [Greek handball championship](/wiki/Greek_Men%27s_handball_championship "Greek Men's handball championship") through the playoffs and became [champion](/wiki/Greek_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "Greek Men's Handball Championship") for the first time in their short history. AEK needed only the win against [PAOK](/wiki/PAOK_Thessaloniki_H.C. "PAOK Thessaloniki H.C.") at Thessaloniki and was losing by one goal (22–21) 30 seconds before the final whistle but with great will and power scored twice (the final goal at the exact second of the final)
and gained the first championship of its history just 6 years after its establishment.[[3]](#cite_note-3) AEK participated in the first round of the [EHF Champions League](/wiki/EHF_Champions_League "EHF Champions League") for the period 2011–12 and also in the first round of Challenge Cup, losing to Lions (Holland).

On 17 March 2012, AEK participated in the final game of the [Greek men's handball cup](/wiki/Greek_men%27s_handball_cup "Greek men's handball cup") against PAOK but lost the game 26–24. AEK participated to the [EHF Cup](/wiki/EHF_Cup "EHF Cup") for the period 2012–13
but was eliminated by [Diomidis Argous](/wiki/AC_Diomidis_Argous "AC Diomidis Argous") in the first round, losing both games at Argos and Athens.

On 3 March 2013, AEK won the second [Greek handball cup](/wiki/Greek_men%27s_handball_cup "Greek men's handball cup") in its history, winning [PAOK](/wiki/PAOK_Thessaloniki_H.C. "PAOK Thessaloniki H.C.") by a score of 27–23 in Kerkyra (Corfu).[[4]](#cite_note-4) The same year on 1 June 2013 AEK won the second [Greek handball championship](/wiki/Greek_Men%27s_handball_championship "Greek Men's handball championship") against Diomidis.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

On 19 March 2014, AEK won the third [Greek handball championship](/wiki/Greek_Men%27s_handball_championship "Greek Men's handball championship") in its history, winning [Diomidis](/wiki/AC_Diomidis_Argous "AC Diomidis Argous") 18–16 in Athens.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

On 29 March 2015, AEK participated in the final of the [Greek men's handball cup](/wiki/Greek_men%27s_handball_cup "Greek men's handball cup") for the fifth time in a row, against PAOK, but lost the game by a score of 27–29. The team finished third in the [Greek handball championship](/wiki/Greek_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "Greek Men's Handball Championship").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

On 14 and 20 May 2018, AEK HC played the final games of [EHF Challenge Cup](/wiki/EHF_Challenge_Cup "EHF Challenge Cup") [2017–18](/wiki/2017%E2%80%9318_EHF_Challenge_Cup "2017–18 EHF Challenge Cup") and became one of the three most successful teams in Greek handball
(including Diomidis Argous and Filippos Verias) in European achievements.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 2020, AEK won its third [Greek Championship](/wiki/Greek_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "Greek Men's Handball Championship").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

On 23 May 2021, AEK HC won the fourth [Greek handball cup](/wiki/Greek_men%27s_handball_cup "Greek men's handball cup") in its history, winning PAOK by a score of 24–22 in Kozani. On 30 May of the same year, AEK HC won the first [EHF European Cup](/wiki/EHF_European_Cup "EHF European Cup") in its history by winning the Swedish club [Ystads IF](/wiki/Ystads_IF "Ystads IF"), this way AEK HC became the most successful Greek club in European handball competitions.[[9]](#cite_note-9) AEK won its fourth [Greek Championship](/wiki/Greek_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "Greek Men's Handball Championship") on 6 July 2021 celebrating a historic treble.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

At the start of the 2022–23 season, the club made several changes in the first-team squad due to last year's unsuccessful results. The first-team changes of the summer transfer period helped AEK to finish at the top of the league in the regular season. The club was drawn to play against Olympiacos after qualifying for the championship finals through the regular season. In an exciting match series, which ended 3-2 in favor of AEK, the club was crowned champion for the fifth time.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In April 2025, AEK handball club lifted its fifth [Greek handball cup](/wiki/Greek_men%27s_handball_cup "Greek men's handball cup") winning [ESN Vrilissia](/wiki/ESN_Vrilissia "ESN Vrilissia") by a score of 43-21 in [SUNEL Arena](/wiki/SUNEL_Arena "SUNEL Arena") at Athens.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
